turns.summary: M1 → A (later corrected mid-lifecycle). M2 (incompleteness): heading-coverage.json registry format. Options: A {spec_file, heading, test} + scenario exclusion; B enforce unique heading text across all files; C leave unspecified. Recommended A.

turns.content: User wants to clarify M2 — asked: Where does the heading coverage file live? When is this update cycle? When does it get committed? How do we ensure it stays as a source of truth?

turns.summary: Explained: file lives at tests/heading-coverage.json (repo-root). PROPOSAL.md line 2118-2119 explicitly defers 'workflow is implementation concern, not specified here'. Meta-test catches drift at check time; update mechanism not codified. The clarification question surfaced a separate lifecycle concern distinct from M2's format question. Offered 3 options: (1) reformulate M2 to format only + file separate M8 for lifecycle; (2) bundle format+lifecycle in M2; (3) drop format in favor of lifecycle. User chose option 1.
Re-asked M2 with format-only scope. User chose A: {spec_file, heading, test} + Scenario: exclusion (Recommended).

turns.content: M2 → A: {spec_file, heading, test} + Scenario: exclusion (Recommended)

turns.summary: M2 → A. Introduced new M8 (lifecycle). Initial options A (spec-author responsibility + meta-test drift catch); B (revise wrapper auto-writes placeholders); C (doctor-static backfill). Recommended A.

turns.content: User wants to clarify M8 (no answer given yet).

turns.summary: Asked what to clarify.

turns.content: You need to elaborate on A. What gets updated, and who actually writes the test?

turns.summary: Elaborated: updates flow to registry entry + the actual test file. Two sub-options within A: A1 (LLM writes real test same commit — tight coupling); A2 (LLM writes TODO placeholder + real test in follow-up — preserves spec-leads-or-keeps-pace separation). Offered 4-way question: A1, A2, B, C.

turns.content: But option A2 is just going to break CI as soon as we push the commit with a to-do entry, right?

turns.summary: Acknowledged correct concern. Reframed: A1 tight coupling (writes real test with xfail if implementation not ready); A2' TODO-with-required-reason tolerated at `just check` but release-gate rejects; B or C wrapper/doctor write placeholder. Offered 3-way re-ask.

turns.content: A1 is a nonstarter too. It's putting logic that is specific to live spec into the revise command to apply to all users.

turns.summary: Acknowledged critical architectural point. heading-coverage.json is livespec-repo-internal (tests/ tree is for testing the skill itself per PROPOSAL.md line 1986-1988). Any skill-level mechanism leaks livespec-specific machinery to all users. Offered 3 options: withdraw M8; narrowly reframe (move lifecycle into livespec's own constraints via python-style-doc-into-constraints deferred entry); keep open but defer.

turns.content: Explain exactly what you mean here: [narrow reframe option]. When exactly are the boundaries of files on disk or sections of files that you are using to segregate these different areas that you are referring to? Everything is going in the proposal now, right?

turns.summary: Clarified file-on-disk boundaries: today, everything is in brainstorming/approach-2-nlspec-based/*.md (PROPOSAL.md, style doc, deferred-items.md). After seed, python-skill-script-style-requirements.md migrates to SPECIFICATION/constraints.md per python-style-doc-into-constraints deferred entry. 'Definition of Done' is a section within PROPOSAL.md. Sloppy gesture at 'move to livespec's own DoD' was really about editing a section of PROPOSAL.md. User's pushback correct: everything is in the brainstorming set.

turns.content: Where are all the other specifications about dev tooling that we have discussed? All of the ones that are not part of the skill but live under the dev tooling directory.

Correct distinction is shipped vs. not-shipped, not PROPOSAL.md vs. not-PROPOSAL.md. M8 options reframed as livespec-repo-internal mechanisms (A2'-repo / C-repo / A1-repo / Withdraw). Recommended A2'-repo.

Apply-phase correction: caught that M1 Option A ('mise-pin typing_extensions as dev-only dep') is self-contradictory — mise makes a tool available to developers only at their shell, but livespec/** imports @override + assert_never at USER runtime. Paused to re-interview with corrected framing.

Re-interview options: A' vendor full ~150 KB upstream typing_extensions + extend license policy to PSF-2.0; A'' self-implement ~10-line livespec/_typing_compat.py (NO license extension needed, but pyright's reportImplicitOverride may not recognize custom decorator by fully-qualified name); B bump Python floor to 3.11.

turns.content: You're saying the library is 15K, but we will replace it with only ten lines of code?

turns.summary: Corrected size (actual typing_extensions.py is ~150KB / ~3700 lines). Flagged pyright-recognition risk for A'' (reportImplicitOverride uses fully-qualified-name matching; custom livespec._typing_compat.override may not trigger). Offered new third option A''' — vendor a ~15-line shim at _vendor/typing_extensions/__init__.py retaining the `typing_extensions` module name so pyright recognizes the import path but without the 150 KB of unused upstream surface. Re-asked 3-way: A' full vendor; A''' shim; B bump floor.

turns.content: M1 (corrected) → A''': Vendor a ~15-line shim at _vendor/typing_extensions/__init__.py (Recommended)
